text: The surgeon begins the thymectomy by dissecting the pulmonary ligament located just above the aortic branch. The branch is encircled and secured with two double silk ties.
The surgeon uses an endogenous vascular stapler to divide the arterial branch from the aorta, initiating the left lower lobectomy.
The surgeon dissects the arterial trunk to the lower lobe, encircles it, and divides it using a vascular stapler loop.
The surgeon uses a harmonic scalpel to dissect and insert the bronchus to the lower lobe, carefully manipulating the bronchial tissue during the procedure.
The surgeon divides the bronchus to the lower lobe using a green reload stapler, completing the separation of this airway structure.

The surgeon performs an inflation test to ensure that the branch to the upper lobe is not included in the stapling process, visually confirming the correct isolation of the targeted bronchial structures.
The surgeon divides the left lower pulmonary vein using a Y-tree load in the J-A stapler, completing the vascular division necessary for the left lower lobectomy.
The surgeon removes the thymectomy specimen from the thoracic cavity using an extraction bag, followed by the standard closure of the surgical site.
